# DESIGN_PRINCIPLES.md — angular-user-search
*Generated by Layer 0 prompt. Reviewed and corrected manually.*

## Coding Conventions
- Standalone components — `standalone: true` on every `@Component`
- `OnInit` for initialization, `OnDestroy` for cleanup — always both if subscribing
- `takeUntil(this.destroy$)` on every subscription — no exceptions
- Explicit types — no `any`, no implicit returns
- Private methods prefixed with no modifier — just `private`

## Naming Patterns
| Element | Pattern | Example |
|---------|---------|---------|
| Components | `{Feature}Component` | `UserSearchComponent` |
| Services | `{Feature}Service` | `UserService` |
| Models | `{Entity}.model.ts`, interface name = entity | `user.model.ts`, `User` |
| Specs | `{source-file}.spec.ts` | `user-search.component.spec.ts` |
| Test descriptions | `method_condition_expectedResult` | `filterUsers_emptyQuery_returnsAllUsers` |

## State Management
No state library. Component owns its state as explicit typed properties:
- `users: User[]` — all users from API (never mutated after load)
- `filteredUsers: User[]` — current display list (updated on every filter)
- `loading: boolean` — true only during HTTP call
- `error: string | null` — null when no error

## Error Handling
- HTTP errors are caught in the `error` callback of `.subscribe()`
- Error message is set on `this.error` — never thrown or re-thrown
- `retry()` method calls `loadUsers()` again — resets `error` and `loading`
- Console errors use `console.error('[ComponentName]: message', err)` format
- Never use `console.log` for errors — `console.error` only

## Testing Approach
- `TestBed.configureTestingModule` with `jasmine.createSpyObj` for `UserService`
- `fakeAsync` + `tick(300)` to advance debounce timer
- `of(mockUsers)` for success, `throwError(...)` for error scenarios
- Assert on component state properties (`filteredUsers`, `loading`, `error`)
- Do not assert on DOM — assert on component state
- 8 tests covering: load, filter by name/username/email, empty query, no match, error, retry

## Design Constraints
- Do not use `inject()` — constructor injection only
- Do not use async pipe in templates where loading/error state is needed
- Do not use `@ViewChild` or DOM manipulation — bind to component state only
- Do not use `ngModel` — reactive forms (`FormControl`) only
- Do not assert on DOM in tests — test component properties
- Do not use `jasmine.createSpy` for services — use `createSpyObj` with explicit methods
